Add /ds:segment-validator command (#3)

* Add /ds:segment-validator command

Validates whether a segment's JSON targeting rule matches its human-readable
description by reading the rule source code in thanx-nucleus via Keystone.

Workflow: parse input -> identify rule types -> read source from Keystone ->
translate rule to plain English -> compare against description -> report match/mismatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: validate paths, bump version, improve fallback handling

- Bump version 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 (new command = minor bump)
- Clarify Step 3 field definitions to check every referenced field
- Add missing file fallback: search repo before reporting unknown
- Frame operator list as examples, defer to source code for full set
- Distinguish absolute vs relative dates with concrete formats
- Rewrite Rule 7: report Keystone unavailability instead of hollow fallback

All nucleus paths verified via Keystone MCP:
- app/models/target/rule/{type}.rb (18 rule types confirmed)
- app/models/target/README.md
- app/classes/target/rule/validator.rb
- app/classes/target/rule/field_validators.rb
- app/models/segment.rb

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Link to Segment Cookbook and Comprehensive Segmentation Attributes
Guide as cross-reference sources for validating segment descriptions
against known patterns.

- Align Rule 4 date format guidance with Step 4 (support relative dates)

- Add "not exhaustive" note to hardcoded rule type list

Regrade all 103 policies with gpt-5.2 + fix co-pay definition rule

- Updated co-pay rule: IRDAI glossary definition alone is no longer flagged as BAD
  (every policy must include the definition by law — only applied clauses = BAD)
- Co-pay enforcement now built into regrade_all.mjs (no separate script needed)
- Concurrency bumped to 15 workers
- 13 co-pay entries moved to BAD by post-processor (genuine applied clauses)
- All 103 policy JSONs regenerated with gpt-5.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix indicator parameter toggle inversion and adopt null convention

The editable and visibility toggles were inverted: they showed ON for
default values (editable=true, visible=true), but ON should mean "an
admin actively overrode the default behavior." This confused operators.

Changes:
- Fix toggle logic: ON = value overrides default, OFF = null (no override)
- Rename "Visible" label to "Masqué" and use EyeOff icon (override = hide)
- Add null-aware IndicatorParams types (future-proof for backend migration)
- Add ruleForApi() bridge in facade to convert null→string for current API
- Add isRuleOverridden() typed utility with RuleField union type
- Add tests for backward-compat string defaults and JSONLogic preservation
- Add backend work summary and update API observations with decisions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch 1120-S PDF rendering from coordinate overlay to AcroForm field filling

Replace pixel-coordinate-based PDF rendering with AcroForm field mapping for
Form 1120-S. The renderer now auto-selects the backend: forms in ACROFORM_REGISTRY
use pymupdf to fill named IRS fields directly; all others fall back to the
coordinate overlay. This makes adding new forms a matter of writing a field map
dict instead of calculating pixel positions.

New files:
- acroform_filler.py: pymupdf-based field filling engine
- formatting.py: extracted shared format functions (currency, boolean, etc.)
- field_maps/f1120s.py: 389-entry map (40 header + 349 form lines)
- dump_acroform_fields.py: discovery script for IRS PDF fields
- test_acroform_filler.py: 33 tests (field map validation, filler, integration)

Modified:
- renderer.py: dual-path render() with ACROFORM_REGISTRY + COORDINATE_REGISTRY
- f1120s.pdf: replaced with IRS fillable version (390 AcroForm fields)
- pyproject.toml: promoted pymupdf to production dependency

commit.message:
Add confirmation flow for long videos in group chats (#2)

* Set up repo for Claude Code Web with CLAUDE.md

- Add .claude/ hooks and settings for web environment
- Add CLAUDE.md with development workflow, learning test process,
  and gotchas
- Change pre-commit hook to run lint directly instead of via Docker

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HLpVRBuMWCKHKQ938MFF5d

* Add learning/contract test infrastructure

- Add learning tests that verify assumptions against real Telegram API
  and yt-dlp, then validate mocks match real behavior
- Add fixtures for real Telegram payloads and video duration data
- Add learning-test.sh script and update-fixtures.ts tooling

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HLpVRBuMWCKHKQ938MFF5d

* Add confirmation dialog for large video downloads in group chats

Videos over 20 minutes in group chats now prompt with Yes/No buttons
before downloading. Features:
- Pre-download check when duration is known from metadata
- Post-download probe when duration was unknown or misreported
- File-based persistence for pending downloads (survives restarts)
- Only the original requester can cancel; anyone can confirm
- Atomically takes pending entries to prevent race conditions

Includes comprehensive acceptance tests and mock infrastructure.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HLpVRBuMWCKHKQ938MFF5d

* Fix PR review issues: error handling, type safety, and test coverage

- Send error feedback to user when confirmed download fails (#1/#5)
- Log errors in safeAnswer/safeDelete instead of swallowing (#2)
- Narrow catch blocks in pending-downloads, log non-ENOENT errors (#3/#4)
- Add callbackQueryHandler wiring test in bot.test.ts (#6)
- Stop mutating memoized VideoInfo; use spread copy (#7)
- Remove stale planning comments (#8)
- Fix hasChanges bug in update-fixtures.ts (per-service flag) (#9)
- Use crypto.randomUUID() for collision-free pending IDs (#10)
- Answer unrecognized callback data to dismiss spinner (#11)
- Fix misleading "don't await" comment (#12)
- Remove commented-out code in VideoInfo type (#13)
- Make postDownload a required boolean (#14)
- Fix import ordering in handlers.ts
- Add formatDuration seconds branch test
- Make webpage_url optional in VideoInfo, set it in getInfo

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HLpVRBuMWCKHKQ938MFF5d

---------

commit.message:
docs: update pinout for server (I2S) and client (DFPlayer) separately

Split the single "shared" pinout into two correct sections:
- Server: LoRa SX1278 (VSPI) + MAX98357A (I2S GPIO26/25/33)
- Client: LoRa SX1278 (VSPI) + DFPlayer Mini (UART2 GPIO16/17/4) + LED GPIO33
Add LoRa RF parameters table (freq, SF, BW, CR, sync word, power)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ACQUIRE command using wrong sample name for existing projects

When an existing project was open, the ACQUIRE command was built with
the user-entered sample name from the dialog instead of the actual
project folder name. This caused the Python server to look for
TileConfiguration.txt in a non-existent directory, silently failing
the acquisition.

Changed buildConfiguration() and saveAcquisitionCommand() to use
actualSampleName/actualProjectsFolder (derived from the tile dir
path) instead of result.sampleName()/projectsFolder, matching what
stitching already does correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ECS Pino compliance: dual-mode output, trace mixin, OTLP delegation

- Dual-mode output: raw ECS NDJSON for production/staging, color-coded
  human-readable format for local/development/test (NODE_ENV driven)
- Replace manual emitOtelLog() with PinoInstrumentation OTelPinoStream
- Replace per-method captureTraceContext() with Pino mixin using ECS
  dot-notation (trace.id, span.id, transaction.id)
- Configure PinoInstrumentation: disableLogCorrelation=true (prevents
  underscore-format duplicates), disableLogSending=false (OTLP path)
- Remove unused useWorker config, dead PinoChildAdapter config param
- Add 7 ECS compliance tests (pino-ecs-compliance.test.ts)
- Comprehensive logging.md update: dependencies, ecsFormat options,
  ECS field inventory, console format spec, OTLP data flow diagram,
  PinoInstrumentation config, reinitialization explanation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix delta_color and sample data for positive demo P&L

- Remove all conditional delta_color="inverse" — Streamlit's delta_color="normal"
  already shows green for positive and red for negative automatically;
  using "inverse" when value < 0 was incorrectly forcing green on losses
- Rewrite sample_data.csv: bull market scenario with 4 CHF-only assets
  (EQCH, CSSMI, IGLD, CSIL), buys at prices below current level,
  2 realized-profit sells — gross P&L +761 CHF (+10.4% unrealized)
- Fix export path in welcome screen: Account → Documents → Request Document
  → Account activities export (not Transactions → Export CSV)

commit.message:
Upgrade ENT scribe system prompts with expert-level clinical knowledge

Replace the minimal 13-line system prompt with a comprehensive ENT scribe
prompt informed by deep research across:
- ENT subspecialty documentation (otology, rhinology, laryngology, H&N
  oncology, pediatric ENT, sleep medicine, facial plastics)
- AI scribe best practices from Freed, Abridge, Suki, Nuance DAX
- Medical-legal documentation standards and E/M coding (2021+ MDM)
- Clinical note quality metrics and common pitfalls

Key improvements:
- Explicit anti-hallucination guardrails with bracket notation for
  uncertain/missing information
- Laterality enforcement (ICD-10 compliance)
- Medical necessity documentation in every Plan entry
- Clinical reasoning capture for MDM support
- Standard ENT terminology, abbreviations, and scoring systems
  (Lund-Mackay, GRBAS, SNOT-22, AHI, TI-RADS, Bethesda, etc.)
- ENT-specific physical exam documentation standards
- ICD-10 and CPT code families for all ENT subspecialties
- ALL CAPS headers and plain-text output for universal EHR paste
- Scribe chat prompt upgraded with consistency-checking and
  laterality/coding awareness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: parallel frame inference + tuned vLLM constants

- ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=16) runs 16 frames concurrently
  instead of serial one-at-a-time (~15x throughput improvement)
- INFERENCE_STRIDE 5 → 10 (halves request count; delivery videos
  change slowly enough that every 10th frame is sufficient)
- VLLM_MAX_TOKENS 512 → 256 (JSON output is ~150 tokens)
- Removed JPEG_QUALITY constant (now handled by Frame.b64_encoding)
- Error handling: collect all frame errors, route to error pathway
  only after the full batch completes (not on first failure)

vllm.service changes (applied directly on GH200):
- --gpu-memory-utilization 0.30 → 0.75
- --max-num-seqs 8 → 64
- --max-model-len 16384 → 4096

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add stepwise MF-toric troubleshooting based on manufacturer fitting guides

Replace generic troubleshoot suggestions with manufacturer-specific stepwise
protocols for 4 MF-toric lenses (Total30 MFA, Ultra MFA, Biofinity Toric MF,
Acuvue Oasys Max MF for Astig). Shows one step at a time with "Still Not Clear?
Next Step" in adjusted view. Adjustments merge so rotation fixes are preserved
when continuing to troubleshoot vision. Improve visibility of param notes and
remove redundant add values from subtext.

commit.message:
fix: increase utterance_end_ms to 1500 and add 3-word minimum filter

- Gives caller 1.5s of silence before firing transcript (was 1s)
- Ignores transcripts shorter than 3 words to prevent partial phrases
  like "So" or "he's been" from triggering the LLM

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stop manually-started EV charging when SOC < 100%

Previously the integration only stopped EV charging it had started
itself.  Now it also checks the actual switch entity state and turns
off manually-started charging when battery SOC drops below 100%.
